results.reason: docker build -t sre-agent-sandbox completed with exit code 0; build log shows successful two-stage export and image tag.
Container started on port 8000 and /health returned HTTP 200 on first poll at 0.012s (within 30s); docker ps showed container Up.
Host-mapped port 8000 served API endpoints successfully: GET /health 200, GET /schema 200, POST /reset 200 with observation payload.
docker images reported size 480MB (<500MB threshold); docker image inspect byte size also captured.

End-to-end Docker flow succeeded: image built, container started, REST client completed full episode to terminated=true (60 steps), WebSocket lifecycle reset->step->state->close worked, and container was stopped/removed cleanly.

commandsRun.observation: Build completed; final log includes naming to docker.io/library/sre-agent-sandbox:latest.
Container ID returned and container entered Up state.

commandsRun.command: python3 health polling script against http://127.0.0.1:8000/health > .../VAL-DOCK-002-health-check.txt
commandsRun.observation: HTTP 200 received on attempt 1 at 0.012s with body {"status":"ok"}.

commandsRun.command: curl GET /health, GET /schema, POST /reset on port 8000 with headers/body captured to VAL-DOCK-003-* files
commandsRun.observation: All three endpoints returned HTTP 200 with expected JSON responses.

commandsRun.command: docker images sre-agent-sandbox --format ... > .../VAL-DOCK-004-image-size.txt && docker image inspect sre-agent-sandbox --format '{{.Size}}' > .../VAL-DOCK-004-image-bytes.txt
commandsRun.observation: Reported size was 480MB; inspect size 102013326 bytes.
REST episode terminated=true after 60 steps; WebSocket reset/step/state/close transcript recorded.

commandsRun.command: docker stop g2-docker-val && docker rm g2-docker-val && docker ps -a --filter name=g2-docker-val > .../VAL-CROSS-005-post-cleanup-ps.txt
commandsRun.observation: Container stopped and removed; post-cleanup listing file is 0 bytes (no remaining container).

frictions.description: Initial REST step call returned 422 because payload omitted required wrapper object; endpoint expects {"action": {...}}.
frictions.resolution: Adjusted client payload shape to match server contract from tests.

frictions.description: Initial REST episode script hit HTTP 409 after reset because session cookie was not persisted between requests.
frictions.resolution: Used HTTPCookieProcessor with cookie jar so /step stayed in the same reset session.
